rulings.Case Title: ALZ LEE, LLC et al VS. SHACUAN SHARON LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Motion To Confirm, Modify, And Or Object To Report Of Referee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, line 2. PLAINTIFFS ALZ LEE, PKL REAL PROPERTY ASSETS, AND STEPHEN & SUSAN LEE LEGACYS' Motion In Support Of Motion To Confirm, Modify, And Or Object To Report Of Referee GRANTED. As recommended in the report, the matter will go to trial as to the validity of the alleged 50 year lease to Sharon Seto & family. Once the validity or invalidity of the lease is established, the sale for partition of the property, 1750 Grant Avenue, Unit D, shall proceed, and an interlocutory judgment shall be entered. = (501/req)

rulings.Case Title: ALZ LEE LLC et al VS. SHACUAN SHARON LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Motion To Confirm, Modify, And Or Object To Report Of Referee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, line 3. PLAINTIFFS ALZ LEE, PKL REAL PROPERTY ASSETS, AND STEPHEN & SUSAN LEE LEGACY Motion In Support Of Motion To Confirm, Modify, And Or Object To Report Of Referee GRANTED. Sale of the property for partition, 1750 Grant Avenue, Unit E, shall proceed. The parties shall prepare and submit to the Court a joint interlocutory judgment, clarifying that any stipulated agreement between the parties shall be subject to court approval before the referee shall be required to act in accordance with it. = (501/req)

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, line 7. DEFENDANTS RALAN WONG AND PATTY HERNANDEZS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED. The judgment in CUD-12-640755 is currently on appeal. In California, judgments pending appeal are not final judgments for purposes of res judicata. (See, e.g., Nathanson v. Hecker (2002) 99 Cal.App.4th 1158, 1163 at fn. 1.) = (501/req)

rulings.Case Title: SOFIA NEW ET AL VS. MANUEL LANGIT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, line 8. PLAINTIFF SOFIA NEW AND JOSEPH NEWS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The Demurrer is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to the second cause of action, constructive fraud, to plead facts showing a confidential relationship between the parties. The Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to cause of action 5, intentional infliction of emotional distress, with leave to amend to plead facts showing outrageous conduct and the severe or serious emotional distress Defendants suffered. The Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to cause of action 6, negligent infliction of emotional distress, with leave to amend to plead facts showing Defendants suffered severe or serious emotional distress. The Demurrer is OVERRULED as to all other causes of action. Plaintiffs' protestations that Defendants are in fact the fraudulent parties represent a dispute of fact not resolvable on demurrer. Similarly, Defendants' cross-complaint pleads with sufficient particularity how, when, where, to whom and by what means the alleged misrepresentations were made, and the existence of a duty based upon the parties' longtime friendship and business relationship sufficient to support the claims for misrepresentation and negligent infliction of emotional distress related to P's alleged misrepresentations that the loan would be repaid. Causes of action 7 through 10 are proper as derivatives of the other properly plead causes of action. = (501/req)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID JARAMILLO ET AL VS. BALWANTSINH THAKO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiffs To Compel The Deposition Of Third Party Witness Brian Johnston; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Brian Johnston.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, line 9. PLAINTIFFS ERIC BOICE, VINETTA BOICE, ELIZABETH CRUZ, ANGELA BRONTLEY AND MARIA ACOSTAS' Motion Of Plaintiffs To Compel The Deposition Of Third Party Witness Brian Johnston; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Brian Johnston. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Deposition of Third Party Witness Brian Johnston is GRANTED subject to proof at the hearing that Brian Johnston was timely served with notice of the motion to compel. The attached proof of service only shows proof of service on the opposing party, not on third party Johnston. The Court reserves the issue of sanctions for the hearing. = (501/req)

rulings.Case Title: THE BETHEL TERRACE, INC. VS. ROBERT MICHAEL ONELLO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, line 11. DEFENDANT ROBERT ONELLO'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS DENIED. Defendant appeared generally in the case on December 19, 2014 by filing a notice of related case and paying his first appearance fee. Moreover, the Declaration of Patsy Gholson establishes sufficient substitute service on Defendant under Code of Civil Procedure 415.20. = (501/req)

rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. MARTIN ENG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, line 12 DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT CONTINUED to May 6, 2015. Both parties failed to submit courtesy copies of their papers, in violation of Local Rule 2.6B. Defendant also failed to provide a courtesy copy of the complaint, also in violation of Local Rule 2.6B. Both parties shall provide courtesy copies of all their filings by April 30, 2015, or the motion will be decided against the non-compliant party. The courtesy copies provided shall reflect the new hearing date of May 6, 2015. = (501/req)

rulings.Case Title: CORINNE KARR C/O DONALD KARR TRUST VS. BRENDA ALESSANDRIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Defendant'S Responses To Discovery Requests; For Order Deeming All Requests For Admissions Admitted; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, line 14. PLAINTIFF CORINNE KARR C/O DONALD KARR TRUST Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Defendant'S Responses To Discovery Requests; For Order Deeming All Requests For Admissions Admitted; And For Sanctions CONTINUED to April 23, 2015. Courtesy copies reflecting the new hearing date of April 23, 2015 shall be provided to Department 501 by 4:00PM on April 20, 2015 or the motion will be taken off calendar. = (501/req)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIANA TRUST, A DIVISION OF WILMINGTON VS. TASNEE BUNCHIEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, line 15. DEFENDANT TASNEE BUNCHIEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED. Defendant's demurrer alleges Plaintiff lacks standing to pursue an unlawful detainer action but provides no explanation whatsoever as to why Plaintiff lacks standing. Defendant also alleges the complaint is uncertain, but fails to actually attach the complaint or explain in any way what is uncertain. = (501/req)
